title: Keep one-file-per-message JSON storage for V1
context: Phase 8 revisited whether Waystation should migrate messages from one immutable JSON file per message to per-thread JSONL. The concern was possible merge friction or many small files as multi-agent usage grows.
decision: Keep the current one-file-per-message JSON layout for V1 and Phase 8. Do not migrate to per-thread JSONL until real volume or merge-conflict evidence justifies a separate migration task.
consequences: Existing message post/list/inbox, validation, MCP, dashboard, and index behavior remain valid.
Git merges usually add distinct message files instead of editing one hot thread file.
Very chatty projects may accumulate many small files, but that is acceptable at the current scale.
Future migration requires a separate task with schema, validation, index, compatibility, and migration acceptance criteria.
